entities.name: SMALLHOLDER IRRIGATION SCHEMES
entities.description: Complex socio-ecological systems that are critical components of agri-food systems, often dysfunctional due to political drivers

entities.name: AGRI-FOOD SYSTEMS
entities.description: Broad systems encompassing the production, processing, and distribution of food

entities.name: POLITICAL OBJECTIVES
entities.description: Goals set by governments or authorities that drive the operation of irrigation schemes, often prioritizing staple food production

entities.name: STAPLE FOOD
entities.description: Basic food crops produced to ensure food security and sovereignty

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The condition of having reliable access to sufficient, affordable, and nutritious food

entities.name: SOVEREIGNTY
entities.description: The right of people to define their own food and agriculture systems

entities.name: PROFITABLE FARMING ENTERPRISES
entities.description: The goal of individual farmers to create sustainable and income-generating farming businesses

entities.name: SCHEME MAINTENANCE
entities.description: The necessary upkeep and repair of irrigation infrastructure and management

entities.name: INFRASTRUCTURE REFURBISHMENT
entities.description: A method of rehabilitation focusing on the physical repair of irrigation assets

entities.name: MARKET LINKAGES
entities.description: The connections between producers and markets, essential for the economic viability of irrigation schemes

entities.name: SOCIAL DYNAMICS
entities.description: The interactions and relationships within the farming community, essential for the functioning of irrigation schemes

entities.name: LIVELIHOODS
entities.description: The capabilities, assets, and activities required for a means of living

entities.name: LOCAL ECONOMIES
entities.description: The economic activity and financial health of the immediate area surrounding the irrigation schemes

entities.name: MULTIPLE INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: A strategic approach using various leverage points to transform dysfunctional systems

entities.name: ADAPTIVE CAPACITY
entities.description: The ability of farmers and systems to adjust to climate change and other stressors

entities.name: CLIMATE SMART AGRICULTURAL SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems that increase productivity, enhance resilience, and reduce greenhouse gas emissions

entities.name: IRRIGATED AGRICULTURE
entities.description: The production of crops using controlled application of water
